text: Voltage sags in LiPo batteries under high throttle often indicate high internal resistance or cell aging.
Icing of the pitot tube causes the airspeed sensor to freeze at a constant value regardless of actual speed.
Intermittent PWM signals to servos are typically caused by connector corrosion or loose ground wires.
ESC thermal shutdown occurs when MOSFET temperatures exceed 100 degrees Celsius to prevent fire.
Compass variance errors occur near large metallic structures or high-voltage power lines due to EMI.
GPS signal loss in clear skies is often caused by 5.8GHz video transmitter interference (VTx bleed).
Structural resonance at high RPM can induce IMU vibration clipping, causing attitude drift.
A sudden drop in motor RPM during hover usually points to a failing bearing or a loose propeller nut.
Telemetry frame loss increases when the aircraft is oriented such that the carbon fiber frame blocks the antenna.
Inconsistent barometric readings in high winds are caused by static pressure port turbulence.
Lidar distance sensors fail in heavy fog or dust due to light scattering back to the receiver.
Rapid capacitor heating on the PDB is a precursor to a short circuit in the 5V regulator rail.
Servo jittering at neutral stick is often a sign of a failing potentiometer inside the actuator.
Excessive current draw without increased thrust indicates a mechanical obstruction in the motor bell.
Optical flow sensors lose tracking when flying over water or repetitive patterns like gravel.
Battery cell imbalance (>0.1V) leads to premature low-voltage cut-offs under moderate loads.
Unintended yaw in autonomous flight is usually caused by a misaligned motor mount or twisted arm.
Over-the-air firmware updates that fail mid-way can cause 'bricked' flight controllers requiring a boot-button reset.
High-frequency motor oscillations are caused by overly aggressive PID D-term gains.
Signal latency exceeding 200ms in the control link is indicative of a saturated 2.4GHz radio environment.
Hydraulic fluid leaks in landing gear systems are often marked by a slow pressure decay over 10 minutes.
Cooling fan failure in the ground station causes CPU throttling and video feed lag.
Parachute deployment fails if the pyro-charge igniter has high resistance due to humidity.
Carbon fiber dust on the flight controller pins can cause mysterious reboots via trace shorts.
A failing BEC (Battery Elimination Circuit) causes sudden loss of servo power while the motor keeps spinning.
